problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display all product information.
ground_truth_hint: Check the spelling of your SQL keywords, particularly SELECT.

problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the email addresses of all staffs

student_query:
SELECT brand_id, brand_name
, non_existent_col FROM brands
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display all brand codes and brand names.
ground_truth_hint: Ensure all columns in your SELECT clause actually exist in the table schema.

student_query:
SELCT item_id, product_id, quantity * list_price AS 'Total Price'
FROM order_items
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the item number, product code, and calculate the price of the product in the item number (quantity is mulitipled by list price). Name the last column 'Total Price'.
ground_truth_hint: Check the spelling of your SQL keywords, particularly SELECT.
reference_answer:
SELECT item_id, product_id, quantity * list_price AS 'Total Price'
FROM order_items;

student_query:
SELECT COUNT(*), Product_name,
    Model_year,
    2026 - Model_year AS 'Product Age'
FROM
    Products
error_message: column must appear in the GROUP BY clause or be used in an aggregate function
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the product name, year the product was launched, and calculate the number of years the product has been on sale since 2026. Name the last column "Number of Years Launched".
ground_truth_hint: Make sure all non-aggregated columns are included in your GROUP BY clause.
reference_answer:
SELECT
    Product_name,
    Model_year,
    2026 - Model_year AS 'Product Age'
FROM
    Products;

student_query:
SELCT product_name AS `Name of Product`, list_price AS 'Price'
FROM products
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the product name and price. Name the columns "Name of Product" and "Price" respectively.
ground_truth_hint: Check the spelling of your SQL keywords, particularly SELECT.
reference_answer:
SELECT product_name AS `Name of Product`, list_price AS 'Price'
FROM products;

student_query:
SELECT
        Concat(First_name , " ", Last_name)
, non_existent_col FROM
        Staffs
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the first and last names of all staffs combined in a single column (separating first and last names with one space, e.g., Fabiola Jackson).
ground_truth_hint: Ensure all columns in your SELECT clause actually exist in the table schema.

student_query:
SELCT 
    CONCAT(first_name, ' ', last_name) AS full_name,
    email
FROM customers
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display a list of all customers. Name the first column "full_name" containing the first name, separated by one space, followed by the last name. The second column should display the email address.
ground_truth_hint: Check the spelling of your SQL keywords, particularly SELECT.
reference_answer:
SELECT 
    CONCAT(first_name, ' ', last_name) AS full_name,
    email
FROM customers;

student_query:
SELECT COUNT(*), city, state
FROM customers
error_message: column must appear in the GROUP BY clause or be used in an aggregate function
problem_description: Write an SQL statement to display the city and state where each customer resides, without showing duplicate rows.
ground_truth_hint: Make sure all non-aggregated columns are included in your GROUP BY clause.
reference_answer:
SELECT DISTINCT city, state
FROM customers;

FROM stores
